textByHash.004e4be8623d7e599f249c21fc4d7c69c8afca6f: This peace treaty specifies the border between Lumbridge and the Cave Goblin realm. Please take it to the cave goblins and tell them I would like to meet with their leader to sign it.
textByHash.01ed4a3cddb512d37687982b502bcc1d45218a58: We are preparing to go to war with the underground goblins over the missing silverware. If you think the cave goblins are innocent you should present evidence quickly!
textByHash.0bea779849ff420747638600c7b85b4888fb8442: They couldn't understand the letter so they took it to one of their elders, Mizaru, to translate. But 'Monster' didn't translate, so they assumed it said 'Monkey'.
textByHash.0f5fa58c1779c6189f78cd30a486efcfd24a4aa8: I am grateful for your help. If you could free up an inventory space, I wish to reward you.
textByHash.10898e6726810f160fd89146accb7fbc4f15281b: That's enough! Get out of my castle now!
textByHash.12f0e1f164e144821a4e15fad06410f0f7a0ddcb: But it wasn't stolen from us. Where could it be from?
textByHash.161ae58b517c4d151cbe55f64c1c632731e40997: Why not speak to Gillie Groats? She seems to handle the milk side of the business now. Maybe she'll get you set up under their permit.
textByHash.18ffe4a852defa7d22751d15865e0a6fc8ed8f84: Back in my father's day, Crandor was an important city-state. Politically it was as important as Falador and Varrock and its ships traded with every port.
textByHash.1b6f9dffd5212dc368be2ff130f928c23dbbc971: It's good to see a visitor from the Dorgeshuun! I am Duke Horacio.
textByHash.1d61dde5cabd86bf090c608d149a2865ad6d9b58: I see I was ill-advised. Unless there is an act of aggression by the cave goblins there is no need for war.
textByHash.1ee4d4520626e3cb947968d2144a052cbf8e7e54: Well, I wouldn't describe it as a quest, but there is something I could use some help with.
textByHash.22737c8266da83a188a9b87d7a204e0e3ac4679f: Hello again, Zanik. I hope you are enjoying your tour. Perhaps someday a human can have a similar tour of your city.
textByHash.22b984020bf58f918e0005e1030d69527083647b: Excuse me while I deal with this...
textByHash.23aa77fe769e5557c9209fccdbc5273d3a23dd54: I welcome Pirate Pete from Braindeath Island.
textByHash.2457dac95f65791e1e30c95637e2baec2a4a2613: Well, you're a braver man than I!
textByHash.27deb7067fe755b8ae41035cfe0e0827c981b4a6: Hmm, I had suspected it. But however much I disapprove of the HAM movement it's not actually illegal. I'll keep an eye on him though.
textByHash.2b7bc67911eb44ce89cbd26e4b0c104a4e90395b: I assure you that the warmongering element has been dealt with.
textByHash.2bb0d69f0051bcc4a36df25236dba1cbd9c2d3f6: Here, take it to the Wizards' Tower, south west of here. Please try not to lose it this time.
textByHash.2eb48312d157dd8974ce476ade56878b7dd7d3d0: I've heard that the blacksmiths are prosperous amongst the peasantry. Maybe you could try your hand at that?
textByHash.32f7a12023615dbb66054b4640e5ab9cc6e8046a: That's a relief. I would hate to see such a promising adventurer cut down in their prime.
textByHash.332f33da8942b22dae682b2ede9e200cb3db29bf: south-west of here. I suggest you deliver it to him as soon as possible. I have the oddest feeling that it is important...
textByHash.39d45097c6f9fefad7f2e1815116e1baba6a709e: I've never heard of a goblin wearing something so well-crafted.
textByHash.3aa48061e966f9970127792602ae6b32f066af8e: I have given you what you can hold. You may wish to visit Diango, in Draynor Village, to collect more rewards in a more convenient setting.
textByHash.3aab8693cb2992c40a4d19ba1ef32244585aa00a: the Wizards' Tower for me? It's just south-west of here and should not take you very long at all. I would be awfully grateful.
textByHash.3b9425b6215b51dd15099ad8d6c638283ee13270: I don't have an infinite supply of these shields, you know. I'll only give one for a truly worthy cause.
textByHash.3d4b423682b02bb6b42b91f84a08a857d756071a: No... I know they've been gaining members but I haven't heard anything else from them. They may be planning something, but then again they may not.
textByHash.3db9ecad15d4de3809d67745df9f50974b137f8a: The wall collapsed because of an earthquake. I'm waiting for a team of builders to come and repair it.
textByHash.3e1edf81c1fe8bb3d230137795e7989b8e6af5f4: If you kill it, for Saradomin's sake make sure it's really dead!
textByHash.421bed02a67f3aa11c1000cf9446f5ac952607c9: Ah. I hope you are enjoying the party?
textByHash.475a2593beef3814d9d766fafed049c5809d2954: Well absolutely, but I'm sure you'll find they're more than reasonable about things.
textByHash.4a7e59532672252a8ead4d463fd00082ff3e37d7: Hmm, perhaps you are right. I will send word to the army to prepare for an underground assault.
textByHash.4b1ccaaf16d8246ba34b71d98eb8b51e60502a58: Don't go digging too deeply into that. Even I have some... commitments that I have to keep to.
textByHash.4ce6f881f9f505fee36014a973180db91cfa4bf4: That's a relief. I would hate to see such a promising adventurer cut down in his prime.
textByHash.4dc542a0a2f449fbfdc77ec043fe90f5a2d42c03: Very wise. There are some monsters that are best left alone.
textByHash.4fc159eb96e3221a01d9cda9052cf97d8f9dc6a3: That's a relief. I would hate to see such a promising adventurer cut down in her prime.
textByHash.50c9b08c8d551f5c79180d3c0d396c01a6edc077: I will not commit troops until I have proof that goblins are behind this.
textByHash.5810f98e61e3e77fc7a2bc5833b7f1cb6683b3a1: You were taking the peace treaty to the cave goblins.
textByHash.5929bd21eddf35d2911b5ec7e49506badbf8e01b: No, all is well for me.
textByHash.599eaf58ae667ffadca6a130c070dd5e994ebff9: I am grateful for your help. As proof of my gratitude, I have arranged a reward for you!
textByHash.5b6b6257f26501e1c65aeb2291b22e6b7c2a5713: So instead of a party invitation they interpreted it as a threat from an organisation called Humans Against Monkeys! They stole some amulets of humanspeak, then decided to come here and put an end to it!
textByHash.5d2baef1e6d074bef3f2829787f4385929c75237: It basically defines the borders between our two societies. It's not usual for two states to be above one another rather than side-by-side!
textByHash.5deb75e98f659737c2d8e0c12498534537269ee7: I really couldn't possibly give you a permit without their approval. This is really their territory.
textByHash.5f7a3e67ca335098818576159c4c6a7ceacb7e45: You were helping to investigate what happened in the cellar. Come back when you've found something out.
textByHash.6180f2924d3f9ffae04734162d35a18cb2be1977: Well, you're a braver woman than I!
textByHash.638892eff31f2aa55c2700b3405654eafa9a8ae1: I welcome Skrach Uglogwee of the Feldip Hills ogres.
textByHash.653ea66e9f231a1b7584ac1c1b9c5e10cbbfe4e8: Yes, he mentioned that to me. But I think he was imagining things. Goblins live in natural caves but everyone knows they don't have the wit to make their own tunnels.
textByHash.6d0c2ad010a517cdea2a2176dd9d1266bcc3ca16: In the process of teleporting, he left the letter behind and these monkeys found it.
textByHash.6ee5335c21dcec56f2ab474465b88d86e07c2f60: Did you take that talisman to Sedridor?
textByHash.6f1aad4fa508fbe1e36aa75e07dc93ee9950beaf: Welcome, Ur-tag. I am sorry that your race came under suspicion.
textByHash.72c349682a95d9566a5c27b51e860aa9ea6655e0: , please find out what you can about this brooch. The librarian in Varrock might be able to help identify the symbol.
textByHash.731d27ef878a59bf7bfed1e23084ce8ef0593d45: It turns out that on the way to King Awowogei, Postie Pete was called off for an urgent request for Molly. Her evil twin had escaped again and she was looking for an adventurer to help.
textByHash.7382e66630a0462b5a047c1da910f6c652bf7f29: I welcome my neighbour, Phileas the Lumbridge Guide.
textByHash.74e362734a83fc6b904f7072ecfb326938e9dd0d: Also, King Awowogei brought gifts from Ape Atoll as an apology for the trouble they caused. But I think you should receive them instead.
textByHash.77c5a09b83c285d2e15f5fbd6037b122da8f88ba: That tunnel could have many origins. I suggest you investigate further.
textByHash.7acb23d6f508b889167685cf42a0dff1d4169053: I welcome the chief of the Goblin Village.
textByHash.7bfbd77ca4eac26b28089bdcf9034fa6c292649f: Take that talisman I gave you to Sedridor at the Wizards' Tower. You'll find it south west of here, across the bridge from Draynor Village.
textByHash.7c10b70d1a3f0b6196b2921bea92dc3c647fe37d: As you wish, stranger, although I have this strange feeling that it is important. Unfortunately, I cannot leave my castle unattended.
textByHash.8421c1b991b6400ad50ab0c1902f1038b3eacf57: , I would still like you to find out more about this tribe. It cannot hurt to know one's enemy.
textByHash.8ad6baca2129bfb57ea60c78e56c4473acb4dda5: The only job I had was the delivery of that talisman, so I'm afraid not.
textByHash.8b95c11c789a8cce61601564c09de8d133784e4b: Milk? Well, the Groats family still have their permit, and I can't start stepping on their toes.
textByHash.8daf8bbb6d01889c41c8a46792d2330e6f679029: I've never seen anything like that before. It doesn't come from Lumbridge. What do you think, Sigmund?
textByHash.8f9bb27d7b1c0294b6f6b2eefedd73637443fda9: Tell Ur-Tag that I wish to speak with him again. I think that our cities could profit from trading with one another, but there can be little trade if he does not allow visitors to enter the city.
textByHash.95f363c998458b79e481e192f30b782749a8d719: Another elder, Kikazaru, found the letter. And once he understood the situation, he went straight to King Awowogei himself. The King is over in the corner if you want to speak to him.
textByHash.9a25c987c6af23a64ed074ee6c30687e98faec2b: ...and finally, I welcome the ruler of Ape Atoll, Awowogei.
textByHash.9da18fb48c87490446de5f710102faec522056e9: So glad you could make it. I really do appreciate you rounding up all those monkeys for us.
textByHash.9dca10b495617eb3e5e734e0259008e576cdb97f: The only task remotely approaching a quest is the delivery of that talisman I gave you to the head wizard of the Wizards' Tower,
textByHash.9edde1150d2ab45b054aaf7982e852995e718955: Ah, well that explains things. One of my servants found it outside, and it seemed too much of a coincidence that another would suddenly show up.
textByHash.a19e66a8a30ad143b8b71c700ec0895214e8cdf2: As you wish. Hopefully I can find someone else to help.
textByHash.a886b67454ec454aeb00d51b03603a21ffb9148c: Ah, that would explain it. One of my servants found this outside, and it seemed too much of a coincidence that more than one strange
textByHash.abe8c53a29632afcb6fea5ffd6e29f1bf2549853: If you really think you're up to it then perhaps you are the one who can kill this dragon.
textByHash.ac5772f9c5da402edf5a7ea9f375c51c2492adcf: Unless it is returned, I am afraid I will have no option but war.
textByHash.acce0640f6ea91b055c7a96f5127ba37502b3525: Well, it's not really a quest but I recently discovered this strange talisman.
textByHash.aee572910bb72d5cea8e16beb4cd46d62d0746a2: The Order of Wizards over at the Wizards' Tower have been on the hunt for magical artefacts recently. I wonder if this might be just the kind of thing they're after.
textByHash.b0a780e963fa2a8cdca85f0d64bc02ebd7f86e06: From the town of Edgeville, I welcome Evil Dave.
textByHash.b0f398ec6e4da32e5200b06584c5e47469168d3d: object would appear on my land in such a short period of time. Please take this to the head wizard at the Wizards' Tower, south-west of here, and don't lose it this time.
textByHash.b40459047c63c276c340f874628f1294500c8991: Now then, child, there is room for caution and experience in politics.
textByHash.b496bd73f55f8ff19c797684ed6642a400018ff8: I think that is everything I have for you.
textByHash.b7f6450a2a690760f7fa4dd2d63f4d2e407d1342: I welcome Selim, acting Spymaster for the Emir of Al Kharid.
textByHash.b8e335f598bb26584edb838541ec9dcaa264aefd: A knight going on a dragon quest, hmm? What dragon do you intend to slay?
textByHash.ba5932f001dbe3fca5571e8fa2456ce73dc61c49: Back in my father's day, Crandor was an important city-state. Politically, it was as important as Falador or Varrock and its ships traded with every port.
textByHash.bc7a34bc45c1f4c0cba41b099006038556c87ee6: I remember my father being very scared. He posted lookouts on the roof to warn if the dragon was approaching. All the city rulers were worried that Elvarg would devastate the whole continent.
textByHash.c1d272bfdfe2fd638d24747e33e4ae0678984535: I welcome Osman, Spymaster for the Emir of Al Kharid.
textByHash.c39966b08733a91d5c5a009653abaea060355019: I remember my father being very scared. He posted lookouts on the roof to warn if the dragon was approaching. All the city rulers worried that Elvarg would devastate the whole continent.
textByHash.c4eaa30b61c9080d70cc6c5eb879c7a108b94062: Did you speak to the head wizard for me yet, adventurer?
textByHash.c863964ddada3efb9e27cce4a8d7c22ced45ecf9: We were recently sorting through some of the things stored down in the cellar, and we found this old talisman.
textByHash.cbd49cf57fc4b0d9d012108aaea7f9560b920cda: It is enchanted to save you in your hour of need.
textByHash.ce4b15f688c58f7753bac203df94d8a47041f681: Of course both our underground systems might expand. Neither state can build new tunnels that are too close to the other's systems...
textByHash.d02f6688c6deb7b2b9a1f96b97cf704d140b9d29: No, no. It's really quite important you speak to Gillie about this.
textByHash.d29666c94fa926197bb64d0d4a6980da78444e6d: Oh wonderful! What sort of business are you looking to start?
textByHash.d472742b421019121df910dc129939395a98bf0a: Thank you very much. You'll find the Wizards' Tower south west of here, across the bridge from Draynor Village. When you arrive, look for Sedridor. He is the Archmage of the wizards there.
textByHash.d9c0bd35961e1a495bde0431e8d0fe2a989c6702: But, one day, when I was little, all contact was lost. The trading ships and the diplomatic envoys just stopped coming.
textByHash.da6e409c8de05ac652aa1858f7d469f8d65579d9: Would you be willing to take it to them for me?
textByHash.dad2f5f8e5d465e1183ccff45e1cffe8276df67e: Actually, something was taken. Sigmund has informed me that some of the castle silverware is missing from the cellar.
textByHash.dbcc6ea9d41bd2bec2df84d6aebba8a0ef1e3e6a: But, one day when I was little, all contact was lost. The trading ships and the diplomatic envoys just stopped coming.
textByHash.dce2827298407f274e619a2cd95422a64596831e: Hmm, very well. I give you permission to investigate this mystery. If there is a blocked tunnel then perhaps you should try to un-block it.
textByHash.dd677a417b06e55efcb37f162709e6a888d47177: I still find it surprising that such a well-crafted brooch would have a goblin symbol on it. Perhaps you should find out more about this ancient tribe.
textByHash.df4564d976c3db25243d1909a5f2af3512e2b4e8: No, let it stay. It can be a route of commerce between our lands.
textByHash.e232941d0cdac5316d2e5151126e3b8b875b1137: Of course. Now you've slain Elvarg, you've earned the right to call the shield your own!
textByHash.e46bde78ae5617965d272bdfd6fdd6dae6e4bdd5: It seems to be mystical and I have never seen anything like it before. Would you take it to the head wizard at
textByHash.e88e858571cda676734c8a01180668404cd16265: , Lumbridge is in your debt. Please accept this ring as a token of my thanks.
textByHash.ebb55934dcb0764b3bdefdaf93ec67ecfd84ccb7: Take care out there. If you kill it...
textByHash.ee664fd2085722fc75d04d54189d37078a9e7440: Everything above ground, within the borders of Lumbridge, belongs to Lumbridge. Everything below ground belongs to the Dorgeshuun. Except for the cellars of our buildings, those belong to Lumbridge.
textByHash.f035ba396993194c5780a4afab5e0283cf61fbb0: Don't lie to me! I knew you were a HAM member but I didn't think you would stoop to this. You are dismissed from my service.
textByHash.f4697d63462da5c7ba20b16970ac433ab34df853: I welcome the chief guard of the White Wolf Mountain dwarves.
textByHash.f92ca801c7fe29e3bdc9e34182e9fcba8b8c88a5: Go speak to Gillie Groats. She seems to handle the milk business in Lumbridge. Maybe she'll get you set up under their permit.
textByHash.fae188e05a852c0ef8b285d25087dfbad10973d3: Thank you very much, stranger. I am sure the head wizard will reward you for such an interesting find.
textByHash.fb168c88d8b8e94553742fbf400a99e637c4afb5: I have nothing more for you.
textByHash.fb348683e10d6ff91e3b636d67b1e4d617c90641: A hearty welcome to Sir Amik Varze, leader of the White Knights...
